# GDELT Project — Data Agreement

## Provider
The GDELT Project (supported by Google Jigsaw)

## URL
https://www.gdeltproject.org/

## Terms of Service URL
https://www.gdeltproject.org/about.html (terms described on about page)

## License
Free, open data. No formal license document but explicitly described as open and freely available for any use.

## Data We Collect
Global event data, media mentions, tone/sentiment, themes, and geographic references via the GDELT Analysis Service (GKG, Events, Mentions databases). Accessed via the GDELT DOC API and BigQuery public datasets.

## How We Use It
Global events and media signal in DRM3 products. GDELT data provides real-time global event monitoring, media sentiment, and geographic event context for data enrichment and trend analysis.

## Commercial Use
Yes. GDELT data is explicitly free and open. The project's mission is to make global event data freely available. Commercial use is permitted and encouraged.

## Redistribution
Yes. GDELT data may be redistributed. The project is designed for open access and reuse. We may serve GDELT-derived event data and sentiment scores via our API.

## Attribution Required
Requested but not legally required. GDELT asks for attribution: "Data from the GDELT Project (www.gdeltproject.org)." We honor this as good practice and academic courtesy.

## Rate Limits
- No API key required for the DOC API
- DOC API: reasonable use expected, no documented hard limits
- BigQuery access: subject to BigQuery quotas (1TB/month free tier)
- Raw data files: freely downloadable, updated every 15 minutes

## Key Restrictions
- No formal restrictions on data use
- GDELT processes publicly available news sources; underlying article content is copyrighted by publishers (GDELT provides metadata/analysis, not full article text)
- Do not represent GDELT-derived analysis as original journalism or reporting

## Last Reviewed
2026-04-02
